subtasks.description: Query SAM database to extract Microsoft licensed users by product/SKU with license count and status (active, inactive, pending)
Query consumption facts warehouse to extract active Microsoft users (those with usage in last 30/90 days) by product/SKU with user count and usage metrics

Reconcile SAM licensed users against consumption active users; calculate delta (licensed vs actual), identify under-provisioned (active users > licensed) and over-provisioned (licensed > active users with threshold). Generate posture classification and provisioning recommendations per product/SKU

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks: (1) SAM query is simple single-source extraction assigned to small tier, (2) consumption query is simple single-source extraction assigned to small tier, (3) reconciliation across two distinct sources (SAM + consumption facts) with delta calculation, threshold-based classification, and recommendation generation assigned to mid tier cross_recon — this naturally bundles the comparison logic and posture decision-making rather than splitting into separate downstream policy agents. No policy_action needed as this is data-driven provisioning analysis, not compliance or governance approval. Minimal subtask count reflects the straightforward two-source reconciliation requirement.

output.sql: SELECT product, COUNT(DISTINCT license_id) AS licensed_users, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN event_type = 'active' THEN user_id END) AS active_users, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_spend_usd, COUNT(DISTINCT license_id) - COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN event_type = 'active' THEN user_id END) AS provisioning_gap FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher = 'Microsoft' AND event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY product ORDER BY total_spend_usd DESC

SELECT product, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS total_users, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' THEN user_id END) AS active_users_30d, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' THEN user_id END) AS active_users_90d, SUM(CASE WHEN event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' THEN amount_usd ELSE 0 END) AS spend_30d, SUM(CASE WHEN event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' THEN amount_usd ELSE 0 END) AS spend_90d, AVG(daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher = 'Microsoft' AND event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' GROUP BY product ORDER BY active_users_30d DESC
